devotionals.title: Casting Your Cares on Him
devotionals.content:
Anxiety often whispers that everything depends on you. Peter reminds us that we can cast all our anxieties on the Lord because He cares for us (1 Peter 5:7). To cast means to throw off, to transfer the weight from your shoulders to His.

Take a moment today to name the specific worries on your heart. Picture placing each one into the hands of Jesus. Pray, "Lord, I give You my fear about ________. I trust that You are stronger than what I fear and kinder than I can imagine."

As you release these burdens in prayer, expect that God will meet you with sustaining grace, even if your circumstances do not change immediately.
devotionals.excerpt: Name your worries and place them into the caring hands of Jesus, who invites you to cast every anxiety on Him.

devotionals.title: Be Still in the Middle of the Storm
devotionals.content:
When anxiety surges, stillness can feel impossible. Yet God’s command, "Be still, and know that I am God" is not a suggestion for the calm days only. It is an invitation to anchor your heart in who He is, even when waves crash around you.

Today, pause for five minutes of quiet before God. Slowly repeat, "You are God. You are with me." Let each breath become a prayerful surrender of control.

Your circumstances may not quiet immediately, but your soul can learn to rest in the faithful presence of the Lord.
devotionals.excerpt: In anxious moments, God invites you to stillness rooted in the unshakable truth of who He is.

devotionals.title: Midnight Prayers for the Anxious Heart
devotionals.content:
Some of our fiercest battles with anxiety come in the middle of the night. The Bible tells us that God gives songs in the night (Job 35:10). When sleep won’t come, you are not abandoned; you are invited into a deeper conversation with your Father.

Turn your anxious thoughts into prayers. For every "what if," respond with "Even then, Lord, You will be with me." Let Scripture become your lullaby, repeating God’s promises until your heart slowly rests.

The same God who watches over the stars also watches over you. He does not sleep, so you can entrust the night to Him.
devotionals.excerpt: When anxiety keeps you awake, turn your "what ifs" into prayers and let God’s promises sing you back to rest.

devotionals.title: Choosing Truth Over Feelings
devotionals.content:
Anxiety often speaks in absolutes: "It will always be this way," "I’ll never be okay." God’s Word interrupts those lies with enduring truth. Feelings are real, but they are not always reliable guides.

Open Scripture and look for one promise that counters your most persistent fear. Write it down and carry it with you. Each time anxiety rises, speak that promise out loud, reminding your heart that God’s voice is stronger than your fears.

Over time, your mind is renewed as you learn to live by what God says instead of what anxiety shouts.
devotionals.excerpt: Feelings can be loud, but God’s promises are louder. Let truth, not anxiety, have the final word.

devotionals.title: You Are Not Alone in Your Anxiety
devotionals.content:
When anxiety presses in, it can feel isolating and shameful. Yet Scripture is filled with people who wrestled with fear and overwhelm—Elijah under the broom tree, David in the caves, Paul under pressure.

God does not shame His children for feeling anxious; He meets them with comfort and strength. Reach out to a trusted friend or pastor today and share honestly about where you are struggling. Invite someone to pray with you.

The enemy wants you isolated, but God places you in a body so you can be carried when your strength feels small.
devotionals.excerpt: Anxiety thrives in isolation, but God meets you through His Word and His people so you don’t have to walk alone.

devotionals.title: Serving Your Spouse Like Christ
devotionals.content:
Christlike love in marriage looks like daily, practical service. Jesus washed His disciples’ feet, demonstrating that greatness in His kingdom is found in humble love.

Ask the Lord to show you one specific way to serve your spouse today without expecting anything in return. As you do, you mirror the heart of Christ in your home.
devotionals.excerpt: Humble, practical service is a powerful way to display Christ’s love in your marriage.

devotionals.title: Pulling Up the Roots of Bitterness
devotionals.content:
Unforgiveness grows like roots that choke out joy. God calls us to forgive as we have been forgiven in Christ. This does not minimize the pain we’ve experienced, but it releases our hearts from the prison of bitterness.

Begin by honestly naming the hurt before God. Ask Him for the grace to move toward forgiveness, even if your feelings are not there yet.
devotionals.excerpt: Forgiveness pulls up the roots of bitterness so God’s grace can grow in their place.

devotionals.title: Daily Bread for Today’s Needs
devotionals.content:
Jesus teaches us to pray for daily bread, not yearly storage. God’s provision often comes one day at a time, inviting us to trust Him in the present moment rather than hoarding for every possible future.

Ask God today for the specific grace you need right now, and thank Him in advance for the ways He will provide tomorrow.
devotionals.excerpt: God meets today’s needs with today’s grace, inviting you to depend on Him one day at a time.

devotionals.title: Finding Peace in Past Seasons
devotionals.content: Looking back, we often see how God carried us through anxious seasons we thought would break us. Remembering His faithfulness in the past strengthens our trust for the future.
devotionals.excerpt: Remembering God’s past faithfulness helps calm present fears.

devotionals.title: Speaking the Truth in Love to Your Spouse
devotionals.content: Healthy marriages are built on honest, gracious communication. Speaking the truth in love means you are both courageous and kind, seeking your spouse’s good rather than winning an argument.
devotionals.excerpt: Let your words build up your spouse, even when discussing hard things.

devotionals.title: Choosing Gratitude in the Ordinary
devotionals.content: Gratitude reorients our hearts from what is missing to what has been given. Even on ordinary days, God is pouring out countless gifts that are easy to overlook.
devotionals.excerpt: Look for God’s quiet gifts today and thank Him out loud.

devotionals.title: Draft: Hope in Waiting
devotionals.content: This is a draft devotional not yet visible to the public.

devotionals.title: Archived: Early Ministry Reflections
devotionals.content: This devotional has been archived and is no longer featured in current listings.

devotionals.title: A Simple Breath Prayer for Anxiety
devotionals.content:
When anxiety spikes in the middle of your day, long prayers can feel overwhelming. A short breath prayer can help you reconnect your heart to God’s presence.

As you inhale, pray silently, "Lord Jesus, You are near." As you exhale, pray, "I give You my fears." Repeat this simple rhythm several times, letting your body and soul slow down together before Him.
devotionals.excerpt: Use a simple breath prayer to remember that Jesus is near when anxiety rises.

devotionals.title: Walking with God Through Long-Term Anxiety
devotionals.content:
For some, anxiety is not a passing season but a long-term struggle. God is patient and present in the long journey, not just the quick fix. He invites you to walk with Him one step at a time, using both spiritual and practical resources.

Consider how Scripture, prayer, community, counseling, and healthy rhythms might all play a part in your healing. God is not disappointed by your weakness; He draws near to you in it.
devotionals.excerpt: God walks with you patiently through long-term anxiety, using many means to bring comfort and growth.
